digest.component_or_area: operation-certificate signing (plan-review / completion-verifier op-certs) on the attestation substrate
propositions: Operation certificates must migrate from per-clone symmetric HMAC self-attestations to asymmetric Ed25519 signatures attributed to the signing environment and verifiable by any third party against that environment's public key.

A project can optionally require a certificate from a specific trusted environment for a gate, pinning trusted-environment public keys out-of-band in .rebar/trusted_environments.yaml on the Gerrit-gated code branch, never the auto-pushed tickets branch.

Required-environment verification checks the signature against the out-of-band pinned public key rather than the certificate's self-claimed DSSE key_id, and certificates bind {ticket id, material fingerprint, merged-log position (a main commit SHA)} to prevent replay onto another ticket or mutated state.

Environment-key rotation uses explicit-SHA era-scoped validity comparing added_at_commit/revoked_at_commit against the cert's bound main commit via git merge-base --is-ancestor, reusing the identity epic's commit-ancestry era rule and key_not_valid_at_era verdict rather than the tickets-branch event resolver.
A merge-gate CI job enforces the required-environment certificate over the merged log by extending the shipped verify-identity gate rather than standing up a parallel merged-log walker.

A trusted rebar-mcp server fetches authoritative ticket state and code-at-commit itself and runs the gate on that, so client-supplied doctored inputs cannot forge a passing verdict, while the legacy HMAC scheme is removed via a named expand-contract migration artifact.
